title: grewia sclerophylla
intro: Grewia sclerophylla, also known as the Australian boxbush, is a species of flowering plant in the family Malvaceae. It is traditionally used in Australian Aboriginal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Grewia sclerophylla include relief from sore throats, coughs, and skin irritations, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate respiratory issues. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, or infusions, and is also used in topical applications to treat skin conditions.
